title: [Non-record] Azure 1xH100 frontier-family engineering run (val_bpb=1.2623)
body:
## Summary
- add a non-record records-folder submission for our strongest verified Azure `1x H100` frontier-family engineering run
- include the exact `train_gpt.py`, exact `train.log`, and `submission.json`
- document the caveats clearly so this is not read as a leaderboard claim

## Why
This is a real H100-side engineering artifact that bridges our earlier proxy-only work and future `8xH100` submission-grade runs. We are submitting it as a non-record public reference because the repo explicitly encourages interesting in-progress or non-record submissions under the `16MB` artifact cap.

## Metrics
- pre-quant `val_bpb`: `1.2591`
- exact post-quant roundtrip `val_bpb`: `1.26233375`
- total bytes: `12,727,250`
- hardware: `1x NVIDIA H100 NVL 94GB`
- exact source commit: `8e8c6d7a23f1bdf26905c0d93e43b5233a45a8ac`

## Important caveats
- this is a `non-record` submission, not a leaderboard claim
- hardware is `1xH100`, not `8xH100 SXM`
- training used a longer engineering wallclock cap (`1800s`)
- the exact post-quant roundtrip metric completed successfully, but the trailing eval tail was interrupted by `SIGTERM` after that metric printed
